title: Elections for the government's chief executive
subtitle: Data by the Lexical Index of Electoral Democracy.
caption: The 2025 world map displays the presence of elections for the government's chief executive across nations, categorized by the Lexical Index of Electoral Democracy. Countries colored blue indicate the existence of such elections, covering the majority of the Americas, Western Europe, sub-Saharan Africa, and Oceania. Orange regions denote the absence of these electoral processes, forming a contiguous band across North Africa and the Middle East, including Morocco, Algeria, Libya, Egypt, Saudi Arabia, and Iran, as well as extending through Central Asia to include China and Myanmar. Greenland appears with hatching to signify no available data. The visual distribution shows a high concentration of electoral systems in the Western Hemisphere and Europe, contrasted by a distinct cluster of non-electoral systems across the northern tier of Africa and Asia.
